heading: Abstract
caption: Figure 1: Comparisons between the proposed order-preserve retrieval-augmented generation (OP-RAG) and approaches using long-context LLMs without RAG on En.QA dataset of ∞Bench. Our OP-RAG uses Llama3.1-70B as generator, which significantly outperforms its counterpart using Llama3.1-70B without RAG.
content: Two-panel bar chart. Panel (a) shows F1 score comparisons across methods, with blue bars for OP-RAG variants (OP-RAG-16K, OP-RAG-24K, OP-RAG-48K) and orange bars for long-context models (Llama3.1-70B, GPT-4O, Gemini-1.5-Pro). Visible bar labels include 44.43, 45.45, 47.25, 34.26, 32.36, and 43.08. Panel (b) shows average input token count (K) for the same methods, with blue bars labeled 16, 24, and 48, and orange bars labeled 117, 117, and 196. Legends identify Llama3.1-70B OP-RAG versus Long Context.

subplots.label: a
subplots.content: F1 score bar chart comparing OP-RAG-16K, OP-RAG-24K, OP-RAG-48K, Llama3.1-70B, GPT-4O, and Gemini-1.5-Pro.

subplots.label: b
subplots.content: Average input tokens (K) bar chart comparing the same methods.

caption: Figure 2: Vanilla RAG versus the proposed order-preserve the RAG. As shown in the example, a long document is cropped into 13 chunks, {c_i}_{i=1}^{13}. The similarity score is appended to each chunk. We retrieve top 4 chunks with the highest similarity scores. Vanilla RAG places the chunks in a score-descending order, whereas the proposed order-preserve RAG places the chunks based on the order in the original document.
content: A schematic diagram comparing two chunk-ordering strategies for retrieval-augmented generation. The top row shows 13 document chunks (c1 to c13), each annotated with a similarity score. Below, the left branch labeled vanilla RAG selects the top chunks and orders them by descending score; the right branch labeled order-preserve RAG selects the same top chunks but keeps their original document order. Colored boxes show the retrieved chunks and their scores.

caption: Figure 3: The influence of context length on the performance of RAG. The evaluations are conducted on En.QA and EN.MC datasets of ∞Bench.
content: Two-panel line chart figure showing the influence of context length (x-axis, in K) on RAG performance. Left panel (a) EN.QA plots F1 score for Llama3.1-8B (blue circles) and Llama3.1-70B (orange triangles) across context lengths from about 4K to 96K; 8B peaks around 16K then declines, while 70B rises to the mid/high 40s and stays relatively high before tapering slightly. Right panel (b) EN.MC plots Accuracy for the same two models over similar context lengths; 8B peaks around 16K then declines, while 70B peaks around 24K–48K and then slowly decreases.

subplots.label: a
subplots.content: EN.QA line chart of F1 score versus context length for Llama3.1-8B and Llama3.1-70B.

subplots.label: b
subplots.content: EN.MC line chart of Accuracy versus context length for Llama3.1-8B and Llama3.1-70B.

caption: Figure 4: Comparisons between the proposed order-preserve RAG and vanilla RAG. The evaluations are conducted on En.QA and EN.MC datasets of ∞Bench, using Llama3.1-70B model.
content: Two-panel line chart figure. Left subplot (a) EN.QA plots F1 score versus number of retrieved chunks, comparing Vanilla RAG (blue circles) and Order-Preserve RAG (orange triangles). Right subplot (b) EN.MC plots Accuracy versus number of retrieved chunks with the same two methods. In both subplots, the order-preserve method is consistently higher, especially at larger chunk counts.

subplots.label: a
subplots.content: EN.QA: F1 score vs. number of retrieved chunks for Vanilla RAG and Order-Preserve RAG.

subplots.label: b
subplots.content: EN.MC: Accuracy vs. number of retrieved chunks for Vanilla RAG and Order-Preserve RAG.

heading: 4.4 Main Results
caption: Table 1: Comparisons among the long-context LLM without RAG, SELF-ROUTE mechanism (Li et al., 2024) and the proposed order-preserve (OP) RAG.
content: Comparison table with columns Method, EN.QA F1 Score, EN.QA Tokens, EN.MC Acc., and EN.MC Tokens. It groups methods into three sections: Long-context LLM w/o RAG, SELF-ROUTE (Li et al., 2024), and Llama3.1-70B order-preserve RAG (ours). Listed rows include Llama3.1-70B, GPT-4O, Gemini-1.5-Pro, GPT-4O, Gemini-1.5-Pro, OP-RAG-16K, OP-RAG-24K, and OP-RAG-48K, with corresponding scores and token counts.
